issue: rotate(text, k) is supposed to shift each lowercase letter FORWARD by k places through the alphabet (wrapping z -> a), but it shifts them BACKWARD instead. rotate('abc', 1) returns 'zab' when it should return 'bcd', and rotate('xyz', 3) returns 'uvw' when it should return 'abc'. Non-letter characters and a shift of zero happen to come out right, which hides the problem. The bug is entirely in main.py -- the alphabet-position arithmetic moves letters the wrong way.
